searchDocs.title: Creating an Instance Pool​
searchDocs.content: local GoodPool = require(path.to.GoodPool) -- Note: forInstance return the same as GoodPool.new local pool = GoodPool.forInstance("Part", function(part) part.Parent = nil part.Anchored = true part.Size = Vector3.new(1, 1, 1) part.CFrame = CFrame.new(0, 100, 0) part.Transparency = 0 part.CanCollide = true end )

searchDocs.title: Getting and Returning Instances​
searchDocs.content: local part = pool:Get() part.CFrame = CFrame.new(0, 0, 0) -- When done pool:Return(part)

searchDocs.title: Cleaning the Pool​
searchDocs.content: pool:Clean()   This will :Destroy all pooled instances.

searchDocs.title: Getting Started with GoodPool
searchDocs.content: Getting Started with GoodPool GoodPool is a lightweight and flexible generic object pool for Luau. It allows you to reuse objects instead of repeatedly creating and destroying them, reducing allocations and improving performance. Here are some quick links to get started using GoodPool: Installation guideAPI DocsBasic UsageInstance PoolingExamples

searchDocs.title: Basic Table Pool​
searchDocs.content: Pooling tables can reduce allocations when frequently creating temporary objects.  local GoodPool = require(path.to.GoodPool) local tablePool = GoodPool.new( function() return {} end, function(tbl) table.clear(tbl) end ) local obj = tablePool:Get() obj.value = 42 print(obj.value) -- 42 tablePool:Return(obj)

searchDocs.title: Pooling Roblox Instances​
searchDocs.content: GoodPool can also be used to reuse Roblox instances instead of constantly cloning and destroying them.  local GoodPool = require(path.to.GoodPool) local partPool, template = GoodPool.forInstance( "Part", function(part) part.Parent = nil part.Position = Vector3.zero part.Anchored = true part.Transparency = 0 end ) local part = partPool:Get() part.Position = Vector3.new(0, 10, 0) part.Parent = workspace -- later partPool:Return(part)

searchDocs.title: Projectile Pool Example​
searchDocs.content: Pooling is especially useful for frequently spawned objects like bullets.  local ReplicatedStorage = game:GetService("ReplicatedStorage") local GoodPool = require(path.to.GoodPool) local bulletTemplate = ReplicatedStorage:WaitForChild("Bullet") local bulletPool = GoodPool.forInstance( bulletTemplate, function(bullet) bullet.Parent = nil bullet.CFrame = CFrame.new() bullet.Anchored = true bullet.Transparency = 0 end ) local function fireBullet(position: Vector3, direction: Vector3) local bullet = bulletPool:Get() bullet.CFrame = CFrame.new(position, position + direction) bullet.Parent = workspace task.spawn(function() while bullet.Parent do bullet.CFrame += direction * 50 * task.wait() end end) task.delay(2, function() bulletPool:Return(bullet) end) end for i = 1, 100 do fireBullet(Vector3.new(0, 5, 0), Vector3.new(0, 0, -1)) task.wait(0.05) end

searchDocs.title: Method 1. Manual Installation​
searchDocs.content: Download the latest release and place it in your project (e.g., ReplicatedStorage).

searchDocs.title: 1. Add dependency​
searchDocs.content: Add the package to your wally.toml:  [dependencies] GoodPool = "thecogumeta/goodpool@1.0.0"

searchDocs.title: 2. Install​
searchDocs.content: wally install

searchDocs.title: 3. Add Packages to your project​
searchDocs.content: Make sure your default.project.json includes Wally packages:  { "name": "YourProject", "tree": { "$className": "DataModel", "ReplicatedStorage": { "Packages": { "$path": "Packages" } } } }

searchDocs.title: Using with roblox-ts​
searchDocs.content: Install the package:  npm install @rbxts/goodpool   Then import in your code:  import { GoodPool } from "@rbxts/goodpool"; const [particlePool, template] = GoodPool.forInstance("Part", (item) => { item.Parent = Workspace; }); ... template:Destroy() particlePool:Clean()

searchDocs.title: Creating a Pool​
searchDocs.content: local GoodPool = require(path.to.GoodPool) local pool = GoodPool.new( function() return { value = 0 } end, function(obj) obj.value = 0 end )

searchDocs.title: Getting an Object​
searchDocs.content: Objects are retrieved using Get().  local obj = pool:Get() obj.value += 10 print(obj.value) -- 10

searchDocs.title: Returning an Object​
searchDocs.content: When you are done using the object, return it to the pool so it can be reused.  pool:Return(obj)   The next time the object is retrieved, it will be reset using the initFn.  local obj2 = pool:Get() print(obj2.value) -- 0

searchDocs.title: Preloading Objects​
searchDocs.content: You can preload objects into the pool to avoid allocations during gameplay.  pool:Preload(50)

searchDocs.title: Cleaning the Pool​
searchDocs.content: To destroy all pooled objects:  pool:Clean()   This calls the destroyFn provided when the pool was created.

searchDocs.title: new​
searchDocs.content: </> GoodPool.new( createFn: () → T,-- Called when the pool is empty and a new object is needed. initFn: (T) → (),-- Called every time an object is acquired. Use this to reset its state. destroyFn: ((T) → ())?-- Called on each object when Clean is invoked. Use this to free resources. ) → GoodPool<T> Creates a new GoodPool.

searchDocs.title: forInstance​
searchDocs.content: </> GoodPool.forInstance( templateOrClassName: Instance | string,-- An existing Instance to clone, or a class name string to create one from. setupFn: (Instance ) → ()-- Called every time an Instance is acquired. Use this to reset its state. ) → GoodPool<Instance >,Instance  Creates a GoodPool that clones Instances from a template. If a class name string is provided, a new Instance of that class is created and used as the template. The template is returned alongside the pool so you can reference it later regardless of how it was created. local ReplicatedStorage = game:GetService("ReplicatedStorage") local partPool, template = GoodPool.forInstance( ReplicatedStorage.Part, function(part) part.Transparency = 0 part.Position = Vector3.new(0, 0, 0) end ) print(template == ReplicatedStorage.Part) -- true local part = partPool:Get() part.Parent = workspace partPool:Return(part)

searchDocs.title: Get​
searchDocs.content: </> GoodPool:Get() → T Retrieves an object from the pool, passing it through initFn before returning. If the pool is empty, a new object is created via createFn.

searchDocs.title: Preload​
searchDocs.content: </> GoodPool:Preload( amount: number-- Number of objects to pre-fill the pool with. ) → () Pre-fills the pool with a given number of objects created via createFn. Useful for avoiding lag spikes on first use. Does nothing if amount is <= 0.

searchDocs.title: Resize​
searchDocs.content: </> GoodPool:Resize( newAmount: number-- The desired pool size. ) → () Resizes the pool to the given amount. If newAmount is greater than the current size, new objects are created via Preload. If newAmount is less, excess objects are destroyed via Clean. Does nothing if newAmount equals the current size.

searchDocs.title: Clean​
searchDocs.content: </> GoodPool:Clean( amount: number?-- Number of objects to destroy. Defaults to all objects in the pool. ) → () Destroys objects in the pool via destroyFn. If amount is provided, only that many objects are destroyed. Otherwise, the entire pool is cleared. Objects that are currently in use (i.e. not yet returned) are not affected.

searchDocs.title: GetSize​
searchDocs.content: </> GoodPool:GetSize() → number Returns the number of objects currently sitting idle in the pool.

searchDocs.title: IsEmpty​
searchDocs.content: </> GoodPool:IsEmpty() → boolean Returns true if the pool has no objects available, false otherwise.

searchDocs.title: Return​
searchDocs.content: </> GoodPool:Return( item: T-- The object to return. ) → () Returns an object back to the pool so it can be reused later. CAUTION This does not reset the object's state. State reset is handled byinitFn the next time the object is acquired via Get.

searchDocs.title: ReturnList​
searchDocs.content: </> GoodPool:ReturnList( list: {T}-- The objects to return to the pool. ) → () Returns a list of objects back to the pool so they can be reused later. CAUTION This does not reset the objects' state. State reset is handled byinitFn the next time each object is acquired via Get.
